---
title: "Private Search"
subtitle: "Choosing the query privately"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: white
    slide-number: true
    embed-resources: false
    code-overflow: wrap
    scrollable: true
    css: slides.css
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
  echo: false
jupyter: python3
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
#| include: false
try:
    ip = get_ipython()
    if "autoreload" not in ip.extension_manager.loaded:
        ip.run_line_magic("load_ext", "autoreload")
        ip.run_line_magic("autoreload", "2")
except Exception:
    pass

try:
    import libdpy
except ImportError:
    %pip install -q "libdpy[notebook] @ git+https://github.com/applied-dp-course/pub_lib.git"
    import libdpy

%matplotlib inline
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
#| include: false
from libdpy.assignment_specific.private_estimation.lecture_figures import (
    make_above_threshold_audit_figure,
    make_baseline_end_to_end_audit_figure,
    make_report_noisy_max_selection_figure,
    make_sparse_group_warning_figure,
    EPS_SELECT,
)

SEED = 42
```

## Recap: private estimation

::: {.fragment}
We can release a private mean via quantiles → clip → noise.
:::

::: {.fragment}
The noisy histogram already served as a **search** tool for Gaussian localization.
:::

## Choosing the query is a release

::: {.fragment}
Which subgroup? Which transform? Which feature?
:::

::: {.fragment}
Each choice made on private data is itself a release.
:::

## Public priors

::: {.fragment}
External tables and domain knowledge propose candidates **for free**.
:::

::: {.fragment}
Validating a public hypothesis on private data still costs budget.
:::

## AboveThreshold — ordered hypotheses

```{python}
fig = make_above_threshold_audit_figure(seed=SEED)
fig
```

::: {.fragment}
Stop at the first good enough — not the best.
:::

## Report-noisy-max — best candidate

```{python}
fig = make_report_noisy_max_selection_figure(seed=SEED)
fig
```

::: {.fragment}
Bounded scores; sensitivity `2/n` under replacement — not the score range.
:::

## Near ties and sparse groups

```{python}
fig = make_sparse_group_warning_figure(seed=SEED)
fig
```

## Minimal baseline predictor

```{python}
fig = make_baseline_end_to_end_audit_figure(seed=SEED)
fig
```

::: {.notes}
Full pipeline lives in the optional notebook section.
:::

## Bridge to logistic regression

::: {.fragment}
Finite menu → continuous weights.
:::

::: {.fragment}
Bounded selection scores → clipped/bounded loss.
:::

::: {.fragment}
Composition and auditing carry over.
:::
